mnemonics: CPU's FAME: Fetch, ALU, Memory, Execute - the core operational cycle of a computer.
keyPoints: The basic structure consists of the CPU (Control Unit and ALU), Memory Unit, and Input/Output System.
The stored-program concept (von Neumann architecture) is fundamental, where instructions and data are stored together in memory.
The bus system (data, address, control) is the communication highway that connects all the major components.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the Control Unit (manages operations) and the ALU (performs calculations/logic).
Thinking the address bus carries data; it only carries the location of where data should be read from or written to.
